key_insight: ARIA reduces LLM “contextual tunneling” by forcing reasoning to follow complete process-structure-property causality, or else fall back to analogical or parametric reasoning.
engineering_relevance: The framework makes AI-assisted materials discovery more trustworthy by producing auditable, physics-grounded predictions and inverse designs for 2D materials.
method: Build a materials knowledge graph with 2,839 extracted process-structure-property relations from the literature.
Route each query through a three-tier cascade: causal reasoning, physics-informed analogical transfer, then explicit parametric fallback.
Condition knowledge use on mechanistic completeness to avoid over-anchoring on narrow retrieved evidence.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates ARIA on forward prediction and inverse design for 2D materials using a literature-derived knowledge graph, with additional gains reported when online literature search enriches evidence.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the completeness and quality of extracted knowledge graphs and online evidence, so sparse or biased literature coverage could still limit reliability.
paper_title: ARIA: A Causal-Aware Framework for Rescuing LLM Reasoning in Trustworthy Materials Discovery
